metadata.name: MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines
metadata.description: The MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines set out technology risk management principles and best practices for the financial sector in Singapore, issued by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS). The guidelines cover governance, risk management, IT project management, software development, IT service management, IT resilience, access control, cryptography, data and infrastructure security, cyber security operations, cyber security assessment, online financial services, and IT audit.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The MAS TRM Guidelines are mandatory regulatory guidelines issued by the Monetary Authority of Singapore for all regulated financial institutions. Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions and enforcement actions by MAS.
metadata.framework_purpose: To promote the adoption of sound and robust practices for the management of technology risk in Singapore's financial sector, covering governance, cyber resilience, and comprehensive IT risk management.
metadata.framework_scope: All financial institutions regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore, including banks, insurance companies, capital markets intermediaries, payment service providers, and other licensed financial entities operating in Singapore.
framework_objectives: Establish sound and robust technology risk governance and oversight
Maintain cyber resilience through defence-in-depth approach
Implement comprehensive technology risk management framework
Ensure secure IT project management and security-by-design
Maintain robust IT service management practices
Achieve IT resilience for business continuity
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, CIOs, CTOs, IT Security Teams, Risk Management Teams, IT Audit, Compliance Officers, and Board Members of MAS-regulated financial institutions in Singapore
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions, enforcement actions, fines, and restrictions on business activities as determined by MAS. The degree of observance of the Guidelines is an area of consideration by MAS in supervising financial institutions.
adoption_approach: Conduct initial gap assessment against the TRM Guidelines requirements
Establish technology risk governance structure with board and senior management oversight
Develop and implement technology risk management framework
Implement controls across all guideline domains
Establish continuous monitoring and assessment capabilities
Conduct regular IT audits and vulnerability assessments
Maintain compliance through periodic review and updates

sections: 3.1 Role of Board of Directors and Senior Management
5.4 System Development Life Cycle and Security-By-Design
6.1 Secure Coding, Source Code Review and Application Security Testing
6.5 Management of End User Computing and Applications
12.1 Cyber Threat Intelligence and Information Sharing

controls.title: Board and Senior Management IT Controls
controls.description: Board and senior management should ensure effective internal controls and risk management practices for IT operating environment.
controls.full_text: Technology is a key business enabler in the financial sector and FIs rely on technology to deliver financial services. It is vital that the FI's board of directors and senior management ensure effective internal controls and risk management practices are implemented to achieve security, reliability and resilience of its IT operating environment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.1
evidence_requirements: Board-approved technology risk management framework document
Evidence of board oversight of IT controls and risk management practices

controls.title: Board Technology Risk Knowledge
controls.description: Board and senior management should have members with knowledge to understand and manage technology risks.
controls.full_text: Both the board of directors and senior management should have members with the knowledge to understand and manage technology risks, which include risks posed by cyber threats.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.2
evidence_requirements: Board member competency profiles showing technology risk knowledge
Training records for board members on technology risk topics

controls.title: CIO/CISO Appointment
controls.description: Board should ensure CIO, CTO, and CISO are appointed with requisite expertise.
controls.full_text: The board of directors and senior management should ensure a Chief Information Officer, Chief Technology Officer or Head of IT, and a Chief Information Security Officer or Head of Information Security, with the requisite expertise and experience, are appointed.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.3
evidence_requirements: Job descriptions showing requisite expertise requirements

controls.title: Technology Risk Management Strategy
controls.description: Board should ensure technology risk management strategy is established and implemented.
controls.full_text: The board of directors and senior management should ensure a technology risk management strategy is established and implemented.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.4
evidence_requirements: Board-approved technology risk management strategy document
Evidence of strategy implementation and progress reports

controls.title: IT Decisions and Risk Appetite
controls.description: Key IT decisions should be made in accordance with the FI's risk appetite.
controls.full_text: The board of directors and senior management should ensure key IT decisions are made in accordance with the FI's risk appetite.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.5
evidence_requirements: Risk appetite statement covering technology risk
Records of IT decisions reviewed against risk appetite

controls.title: Technology Risk Culture
controls.description: Board should cultivate strong culture of technology risk awareness at all levels.
controls.full_text: Given that technology underpins many of the operations and services offered by an FI, the board of directors and senior management should set the tone from the top and cultivate a strong culture of technology risk awareness and management at all levels of staff within the FI.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.6
evidence_requirements: Evidence of tone-from-the-top communications on technology risk

controls.title: Board Responsibilities for Technology Risk
controls.description: Board responsibilities including risk framework establishment, oversight function, authority, risk appetite statement, regular reviews.
controls.full_text: The board of directors or a committee delegated by it, is responsible for: ensuring a sound and robust risk management framework is established; ensuring there is a technology risk management function; giving senior executives sufficient authority and resources; approving the risk appetite and risk tolerance statement; undertaking regular reviews; assessing management competencies; and ensuring an independent audit function.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.7
evidence_requirements: Board charter or terms of reference for technology risk oversight
Risk appetite and tolerance statement approved by the board
Board meeting minutes discussing technology risk management

controls.title: Senior Management Responsibilities
controls.description: Senior management responsibilities for establishing and managing the technology risk framework.
controls.full_text: Senior management is responsible for: establishing the technology risk management framework and strategy; managing technology risks; ensuring sound policies, standards and procedures; ensuring roles and responsibilities are delineated; and apprising the board of salient technology risk developments.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.1.8
evidence_requirements: Senior management roles and responsibilities matrix for technology risk
Reports from senior management to board on technology risk developments

controls.title: Technology Risk Policies and Standards
controls.description: Establish policies, standards and procedures incorporating industry standards for managing technology risks.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish policies, standards and procedures and, where appropriate, incorporate industry standards and best practices to manage technology risks and safeguard information assets in the FI. The policies, standards and procedures should also be regularly reviewed and updated.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.2.1
evidence_requirements: Technology risk management policies and standards
Policy review and update records showing regular updates

controls.title: Deviation Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risks from deviations should be reviewed and assessed, approved by senior management.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure risks associated with deviations are thoroughly reviewed and assessed. The risk assessment should be approved by senior management. Approved deviations should be reviewed periodically.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.2.2
evidence_requirements: Senior management approval records for policy deviations

controls.title: Compliance Verification Processes
controls.description: Compliance processes to verify adherence to policies, standards and procedures.
controls.full_text: Compliance processes should be implemented to verify that policies, standards and procedures are adhered to. These include follow-up processes for non-compliance.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.2.3

controls.title: Information Asset Management
controls.description: Establish information asset management practices including identification, classification, ownership.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish information asset management practices that include: identification of information assets; classification based on security classification or criticality; ownership and roles/responsibilities; and establishment of policies to manage assets according to their classification.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.3.1

controls.title: Information Asset Inventory
controls.description: Maintain inventory of all information assets, reviewed periodically.
controls.full_text: The FI should maintain an inventory of all its information assets. The inventory should be reviewed periodically and updated whenever there are changes.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.3.2
evidence_requirements: Records of periodic inventory reviews and updates

controls.title: Third Party Service Risk Awareness
controls.description: Third party services provisioned using IT may adversely impact operations.
controls.full_text: The use of certain third party services by FIs may not always constitute outsourcing. However, as many of these services are provisioned or delivered using IT, or may involve confidential or sensitive customer information, the FI's operations may be adversely impacted if there is a system failure or security breach at the third party.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.4.1
evidence_requirements: Third party service risk assessment documentation
Registry of third party IT services

controls.title: Third Party Risk Assessment
controls.description: Assess and manage technology risks at third parties before contractual agreements.
controls.full_text: The FI should assess and manage its exposure to technology risks that may affect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the IT systems and data at the third party before entering into a contractual agreement or partnership.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.4.2
evidence_requirements: Due diligence reports for third party service providers

controls.title: Third Party Ongoing Monitoring
controls.description: Ensure third party employs high standard of care for data and system protection.
controls.full_text: On an ongoing basis, the FI should ensure the third party employs a high standard of care and diligence in protecting data confidentiality and integrity as well as ensuring system resilience.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.4.3
evidence_requirements: Third party monitoring reports and assessments
Service level agreements with security requirements

controls.title: IT Personnel Competency
controls.description: Ensure personnel have requisite competence and skills for IT functions.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure personnel, including contractors and service providers, have the requisite level of competence and skills to perform the IT functions and manage technology risks.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.5.1

controls.title: Personnel Background Checks
controls.description: Background checks on personnel with access to data and IT systems.
controls.full_text: Insider threat is considered one of the risks to an organisation. A background check on personnel who has access to the FI's data and IT systems should be performed to minimise this risk.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.5.2
evidence_requirements: Records of completed background checks for IT personnel

controls.title: IT Security Awareness Training
controls.description: Comprehensive IT security awareness training programme for all staff.
controls.full_text: A comprehensive IT security awareness training programme should be established to maintain a high level of awareness among all staff in the FI. The content should include the prevailing cyber threat landscape, IT security policies and standards, and individual's responsibility to safeguard information assets.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 3.6.1
evidence_requirements: IT security awareness training programme materials
Training completion records for all staff

controls.title: Technology Risk Management Framework
controls.description: Establish and implement a technology risk management framework covering risk identification, assessment, treatment, monitoring.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish and implement a technology risk management framework to identify, assess, treat, and monitor technology risks arising from the use of information assets to support its operations and delivery of services.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.1.1
evidence_requirements: Evidence of framework implementation across the organisation

controls.title: Enterprise Risk Integration
controls.description: Risk management framework should be integrated with enterprise risk management.
controls.full_text: The FI's technology risk management framework should be integrated with its enterprise risk management framework. The technology risk management framework should be subject to regular review and update.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.1.2
evidence_requirements: Integration documentation between technology and enterprise risk frameworks

controls.title: Risk Identification
controls.description: Identify and maintain a list of significant technology risks.
controls.full_text: The FI should identify and maintain a list of significant risks that may impact the operations of information assets. The identification should be supported by an analysis of the FI's threat landscape to ascertain threats and vulnerabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.2.1
evidence_requirements: Technology risk register with identified risks

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: Assess risks based on threat, vulnerability, and impact.
controls.full_text: Risk assessments should be conducted to determine the level of risk exposure based on the assessment of the likelihood and the adverse impact of a risk event materialising. The FI should identify risks that exceed its risk appetite or tolerance.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.3.1

controls.title: Risk Treatment Options
controls.description: Select appropriate risk treatment options - mitigate, transfer, avoid, accept.
controls.full_text: Risk treatment involves selecting one or more options to modify the risk. The FI should select the most appropriate risk treatment option based on its risk appetite and the risk assessment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.4.1
evidence_requirements: Risk treatment plans for identified risks
Documentation of risk treatment decisions and rationale

controls.title: Residual Risk Assessment
controls.description: Assess whether residual risks are at acceptable level after applying mitigating measures.
controls.full_text: As there are residual risks from threats and vulnerabilities which cannot be fully eliminated, the FI should assess whether risks have been reduced to an acceptable level after applying the mitigating measures.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.4.2

controls.title: Technology Risk Insurance
controls.description: Take insurance cover for insurable technology risks.
controls.full_text: The FI should take insurance cover for various insurable technology risks to reduce financial impact such as recovery and restitution costs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.4.3

controls.title: IT Control Effectiveness Monitoring
controls.description: Assess and monitor design and operating effectiveness of IT controls.
controls.full_text: The FI should institute a process for assessing and monitoring the design and operating effectiveness of IT controls against identified risks.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.5.1

controls.title: Technology Risk Register
controls.description: Maintain risk register, report significant risks to board and senior management.
controls.full_text: A risk register should be maintained to facilitate the monitoring and reporting of technology risks. Significant risks should be monitored closely and reported to the board and senior management.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.5.2
evidence_requirements: Risk reports submitted to board and senior management

controls.title: Technology Risk Metrics
controls.description: Develop technology risk metrics to highlight highest risk exposure.
controls.full_text: Technology risk metrics should be developed to highlight information assets that have the highest risk exposure. The FI should take into account risk events, audit observations, and regulatory requirements.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 4.5.3
evidence_requirements: Technology risk metrics dashboard and reports
Key risk indicator definitions and thresholds

controls.title: Project Management Framework
controls.description: Establish project management framework for consistency in practices.
controls.full_text: A project management framework should be established to ensure consistency in project management practices, and delivery of outcomes that meets project objectives and requirements.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.1.1

controls.title: IT Project Plans
controls.description: Detailed IT project plans for all IT projects.
controls.full_text: Detailed IT project plans should be established for all IT projects. An IT project plan should set out the scope, activities, milestones and deliverables.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.1.2
evidence_requirements: Sample IT project plans with scope, milestones, deliverables

controls.title: Project Documentation Management
controls.description: Maintain and approve key documentation in IT project lifecycle.
controls.full_text: Key documentation in the IT project life cycle, including feasibility analysis, cost-benefit analysis, business case analysis, project plan, and implementation plan, should be maintained and approved.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.1.3
evidence_requirements: Project documentation repository with approval records
Business case and feasibility analysis examples

controls.title: Project Risk Management
controls.description: Risk management process for project risks throughout lifecycle.
controls.full_text: A risk management process should be established to identify, assess, treat and monitor the attendant risks throughout the project life cycle.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.1.4
evidence_requirements: Project risk registers and mitigation plans

controls.title: Project Steering Committee
controls.description: Steering committee for large and complex projects.
controls.full_text: For large and complex projects, a project steering committee consisting of key stakeholders should be formed to provide direction, guidance and oversight.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.2.1
evidence_requirements: Project steering committee charter and terms of reference

controls.title: Issue Escalation Process
controls.description: Escalation of unresolved risks and issues to steering committee.
controls.full_text: Risks and issues which cannot be resolved at the project management level should be escalated to the project steering committee and senior management.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.2.2
evidence_requirements: Records of escalated issues and resolutions

controls.title: Vendor Evaluation Standards
controls.description: Standards and procedures for vendor evaluation and selection.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish standards and procedures for vendor evaluation and selection to ensure the selected vendor is qualified and able to meet project requirements.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.3.1

controls.title: Vendor Development Practice Assessment
controls.description: Assess vendor's software development and quality assurance practices.
controls.full_text: The FI should assess the robustness of the vendor's software development and quality assurance practices, and ensure stringent security practices are in place.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.3.2

controls.title: COTS Security Assessment
controls.description: Risk assessment for COTS solutions not meeting security requirements.
controls.full_text: If a project involves a COTS solution that does not meet the FI's security requirements, the FI should assess the risks and ensure adequate mitigating controls are implemented.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.3.3
evidence_requirements: Compensating controls documentation for COTS solutions

controls.title: Source Code Escrow
controls.description: Source code escrow agreement for critical acquired software.
controls.full_text: The FI should assess if a source code escrow agreement should be in place, based on the criticality of the acquired software.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.3.4

controls.title: SDLC Framework
controls.description: Establish framework to manage system development life cycle.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a framework to manage its system development life cycle (SDLC). The framework should clearly define the processes, procedures and controls in each phase.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.4.1

controls.title: Security-by-Design
controls.description: Security-by-design approach building security in every SDLC phase.
controls.full_text: The security-by-design approach refers to building security in every phase of the SDLC in order to minimise system vulnerabilities and reduce the attack surface.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.4.2
evidence_requirements: Security evaluation records across SDLC phases

controls.title: SDLC Security Requirements
controls.description: Security requirements covering access control, authentication, data integrity, logging.
controls.full_text: Security requirements should minimally cover key control areas such as access control, authentication, authorisation, data integrity and confidentiality, system activity logging, security event tracking and exception handling.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.4.3
evidence_requirements: Security requirements specifications for IT projects
Security controls checklist for SDLC phases

controls.title: Security Function in SDLC
controls.description: Involve IT security function in each SDLC phase.
controls.full_text: The SDLC should, where relevant, involve the IT security function in each phase of the life cycle.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.4.4
evidence_requirements: SDLC process showing security function involvement
Security sign-off records for each SDLC phase

controls.title: System Requirements Analysis
controls.description: Identify and document functional requirements including performance and security.
controls.full_text: The FI should identify, define and document the functional requirements for the IT system. Key requirements such as system performance, resilience and security controls should also be established.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.5.1

controls.title: Security Threat Assessment
controls.description: Assess potential threats and risks to determine acceptable security level.
controls.full_text: In establishing the security requirements, the FI should assess the potential threats and risks related to the IT system, and determine the acceptable level of security.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.5.2
evidence_requirements: System threat and risk assessment reports
Security requirements based on threat assessment

controls.title: Architecture Design Review
controls.description: Review proposed architecture and design including IT controls.
controls.full_text: As part of the design phase, the FI should review the proposed architecture and design of the IT system, including the IT controls to be built into the system.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.6.1

controls.title: Implementation Verification
controls.description: Verify system requirements are met by design and implementation.
controls.full_text: The FI should verify that system requirements are met by the current system design and implementation. Any changes or deviations should be endorsed by relevant stakeholders.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.6.2

controls.title: Expert Design Review
controls.description: Engage domain experts including security specialists in design review.
controls.full_text: Relevant domain experts should be engaged to participate in the design review. The security design and architecture should be reviewed by IT security specialists.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.6.3
evidence_requirements: Security design review reports by specialists
Expert engagement records for design reviews

controls.title: System Testing Methodology
controls.description: Testing methodology covering business logic, function, security, performance.
controls.full_text: A methodology for system testing should be established. The scope should cover business logic, system function, security controls and system performance under various conditions.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.7.1
evidence_requirements: Test plans covering security and performance

controls.title: Requirements Traceability
controls.description: Trace requirements during testing, ensure coverage by test cases.
controls.full_text: The FI should trace the requirements during the testing phase, and ensure each requirement is covered by appropriate test cases.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.7.2

controls.title: Test Environment Separation
controls.description: Maintain separate testing environments, restrict access.
controls.full_text: The FI should maintain separate physical or logical environments for unit, system integration and user acceptance testing, and restrict access to each environment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.7.3
evidence_requirements: Access control records for test environments

controls.title: Regression Testing
controls.description: Regression testing for changes to existing IT systems.
controls.full_text: The FI should perform regression testing for changes to an existing IT system to validate continued proper function after changes.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.7.4

controls.title: Test Issue Management
controls.description: Track and address issues from testing, report major issues.
controls.full_text: Issues identified from testing should be properly tracked and addressed. Major issues should be reported to the steering committee and addressed prior to production deployment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.7.5

controls.title: Test Documentation
controls.description: Document test results in test report, signed off by stakeholders.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure the results of all testing are documented in the test report, and signed off by the relevant stakeholders.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.7.6

controls.title: Quality Attributes Definition
controls.description: Define quality attributes and assessment metrics during project planning.
controls.full_text: During project planning, the FI should define the expected quality attributes and the assessment metrics for the project deliverables.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.8.1

controls.title: Independent Quality Assurance
controls.description: Independent QA function for project activities and deliverables.
controls.full_text: Quality assurance should be performed by an independent quality assurance function to ensure project activities and deliverables comply with policies.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 5.8.2

controls.title: Secure Coding Standards
controls.description: Adopt standards on secure coding, source code review and application security testing.
controls.full_text: The FI should adopt standards on secure coding, source code review and application security testing to minimise bugs and vulnerabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.1

controls.title: Secure Coding Practices
controls.description: Standards covering secure programming, input validation, output encoding, access controls.
controls.full_text: The secure coding and source code review standards should cover areas such as secure programming practices, input validation, output encoding, access controls, authentication, cryptographic practices, and error handling.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.2
evidence_requirements: Secure coding guidelines covering all required areas

controls.title: Third Party Code Management
controls.description: Policy on use of third party and open-source software codes.
controls.full_text: A policy and procedure on the use of third party and open-source software codes should be established to ensure these codes are subject to review and testing.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.3
evidence_requirements: Open-source and third party software usage policy
Third party code review and approval records

controls.title: Dependency Vulnerability Tracking
controls.description: Track updates and reported vulnerabilities for third party codes.
controls.full_text: The FI should keep track of updates and reported vulnerabilities for third party and open-source software codes incorporated in its software.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.4
evidence_requirements: Third party software vulnerability tracking records

controls.title: Developer Security Training
controls.description: Ensure developers are trained in secure coding.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure its software developers are trained or have the necessary knowledge and skills to apply the secure coding and application security standards.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.5

controls.title: Application Security Testing Strategy
controls.description: Comprehensive application security testing strategy (SAST, DAST, IAST).
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a comprehensive strategy to perform application security validation and testing using a mixture of static, dynamic and interactive security testing methods.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.6
evidence_requirements: Application security testing strategy and procedures
SAST/DAST/IAST tool deployment and scan reports

controls.title: Security Defect Remediation
controls.description: Track issues from code review and security testing, remediate before deployment.
controls.full_text: All issues and software defects discovered from source code review and application security testing should be tracked. Major issues should be remediated before production deployment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.1.7

controls.title: Agile Security Integration
controls.description: Incorporate SDLC and security-by-design in Agile development.
controls.full_text: When adopting Agile software development methods, the FI should continue to incorporate the necessary SDLC and security-by-design principles throughout its Agile process.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.2.1
evidence_requirements: Agile development process with security integration points

controls.title: Agile Secure Coding
controls.description: Apply secure coding standards during Agile development.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure secure coding, source code review and application security testing standards are applied during Agile software development.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.2.2
evidence_requirements: Secure coding application in Agile sprints

controls.title: DevSecOps Alignment
controls.description: Align DevSecOps with SDLC framework and IT service management.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure its DevSecOps activities and processes are aligned with its SDLC framework and IT service management processes.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.3.1
evidence_requirements: Alignment documentation with SDLC and ITSM

controls.title: DevSecOps Security Controls
controls.description: Security measures and segregation of duties in DevSecOps.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement adequate security measures and enforce segregation of duties for software development, testing and release functions in DevSecOps.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.3.2
evidence_requirements: Segregation of duties matrix for CI/CD pipeline

controls.title: API Security Safeguards
controls.description: Establish safeguards for API development and provisioning.
controls.full_text: It is important for the FI to establish adequate safeguards to manage the development and provisioning of APIs for secure delivery of services.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.1

controls.title: Third Party API Vetting
controls.description: Vetting process for third parties connecting via APIs.
controls.full_text: A well-defined vetting process should be implemented for assessing third parties' suitability in connecting to the FI via APIs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.2
evidence_requirements: Third party API access vetting procedures

controls.title: API Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risk assessment before allowing third party API connections.
controls.full_text: The FI should perform a risk assessment before allowing third parties to connect to its IT systems via APIs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.3
evidence_requirements: Data sensitivity classification for API exchanges

controls.title: API Security Standards
controls.description: Security standards for API design including key/token protection.
controls.full_text: Security standards for designing and developing secure APIs should be established including measures to protect API keys or access tokens.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.4

controls.title: API Data Encryption
controls.description: Strong encryption for sensitive data through APIs.
controls.full_text: Strong encryption standards and key management controls should be adopted to secure transmission of sensitive data through APIs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.5

controls.title: API Security Testing
controls.description: Security screening and testing of APIs, log access sessions.
controls.full_text: A robust security screening and testing of the API should be performed. The FI should log the access sessions by third parties.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.6

controls.title: API Monitoring
controls.description: Real-time monitoring for API usage and suspicious activities.
controls.full_text: Detective measures such as real-time monitoring and alerting should be instituted to provide visibility of API usage and detect suspicious activities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.7
evidence_requirements: API monitoring dashboard and alert configurations

controls.title: API Capacity and DoS Protection
controls.description: System capacity for high API volumes, mitigate DoS attacks.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure adequate system capacity for high volumes of API call requests, and implement measures to mitigate DoS attacks.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.4.8
evidence_requirements: DoS mitigation controls for API endpoints

controls.title: Shadow IT Management
controls.description: Control shadow IT and end user computing as part of information assets.
controls.full_text: Shadow IT should be managed as part of the FI's information assets.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.5.1
evidence_requirements: Shadow IT inventory and risk assessment

controls.title: Shadow IT Controls
controls.description: Measures to control and monitor shadow IT use.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish measures to control and monitor the use of shadow IT in its environment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.5.2
evidence_requirements: Shadow IT monitoring tools and procedures

controls.title: End User Application Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risk assessment for end user developed or acquired applications.
controls.full_text: A process should be established to assess the risk of end user developed or acquired applications, and ensure appropriate controls are implemented.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 6.5.3
evidence_requirements: End user application risk assessment procedures

controls.title: IT Service Management Framework
controls.description: Robust ITSM framework for supporting IT operations.
controls.full_text: A robust IT service management framework is essential for supporting IT services and operations, tracking information assets, managing changes, responding to incidents.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.1.1

controls.title: Configuration Management
controls.description: Configuration management for hardware and software information.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement a configuration management process to maintain accurate information of its hardware and software.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.2.1

controls.title: Configuration Review
controls.description: Review and verify configuration information regularly.
controls.full_text: The FI should review and verify the configuration information of its hardware and software on a regular basis.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.2.2

controls.title: Technology Refresh
controls.description: Avoid outdated/unsupported hardware or software, monitor EOS dates.
controls.full_text: The FI should avoid using outdated and unsupported hardware or software, and closely monitor end-of-support dates.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.3.1

controls.title: Refresh Planning
controls.description: Technology refresh plan before reaching EOS.
controls.full_text: A technology refresh plan for the replacement of hardware and software should be developed before they reach EOS.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.3.2

controls.title: EOS Dispensation
controls.description: Management dispensation for continued use of outdated systems.
controls.full_text: The FI should obtain dispensation from management for continued use of outdated and unsupported hardware and software.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.3.3
evidence_requirements: Management dispensation records for EOS systems

controls.title: Patch Management Process
controls.description: Patch management process with timeframes based on criticality.
controls.full_text: A patch management process should be established to ensure applicable patches are implemented within a timeframe commensurate with criticality.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.4.1

controls.title: Patch Testing
controls.description: Test patches before applying to production.
controls.full_text: Patches should be tested before they are applied to production to ensure compatibility with existing IT systems.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.4.2
evidence_requirements: Patch test results before production deployment

controls.title: Change Management Process
controls.description: Change management process for assessing, testing, reviewing, approving changes.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a change management process to ensure changes to information assets are assessed, tested, reviewed and approved before implementation.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.5.1
evidence_requirements: Change request records with approval evidence

controls.title: Change Impact Analysis
controls.description: Risk and impact analysis before implementing changes.
controls.full_text: A risk and impact analysis of the change to an information asset should be conducted before implementing the change.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.5.2
evidence_requirements: Change risk and impact analysis reports

controls.title: Change Testing
controls.description: Test all changes in test environment with approved test plans.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure all changes are adequately tested in the test environment with approved test plans.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.5.3
evidence_requirements: Change test plans with management approval
Change test results and sign-off records

controls.title: Change Advisory Board
controls.description: Change advisory board for approving and prioritizing changes.
controls.full_text: A change advisory board comprising key stakeholders should be formed to approve and prioritise changes.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.5.4
evidence_requirements: CAB meeting minutes and change approval records

controls.title: Change Rollback Planning
controls.description: Backup before change implementation, establish rollback plan.
controls.full_text: The FI should perform a backup prior to change implementation, and establish a rollback plan to revert if a problem arises.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.5.5

controls.title: Emergency Change Procedures
controls.description: Emergency change procedures with defined authorisers.
controls.full_text: The FI should clearly define procedures for assessing, approving and implementing emergency changes.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.5.6

controls.title: Release Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Segregation of duties in software release process.
controls.full_text: Segregation of duties in the software release process should be practised to ensure no single individual can develop, compile and move software codes.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.6.1
evidence_requirements: Software release process with segregation of duties

controls.title: Release Integrity Controls
controls.description: Controls for traceability and integrity of software between environments.
controls.full_text: Controls should be implemented to maintain traceability and integrity for all software codes moved between IT environments.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.6.2

controls.title: Incident Management Framework
controls.description: Incident management framework for restoring affected IT services.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish an incident management framework with the objective of restoring affected IT services as quickly as possible.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.1

controls.title: Incident Response Resources
controls.description: Sufficient resources for incident response and recovery.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure sufficient resources are available to facilitate and support incident response and recovery.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.2
evidence_requirements: Incident response team roster and contact list

controls.title: Incident Handling Procedures
controls.description: Framework covering processes, evidence protection, and roles for incidents.
controls.full_text: The framework should cover the process and procedure for handling IT incidents, maintenance of supporting evidence, and roles and responsibilities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.3

controls.title: System Event Monitoring
controls.description: Configure system events/alerts for early indication of issues.
controls.full_text: The FI should configure system events or alerts to provide early indication of issues affecting IT systems.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.4
evidence_requirements: Alert threshold settings and escalation procedures

controls.title: Major Incident Escalation
controls.description: Apprise senior management of major incidents.
controls.full_text: The FI should regularly apprise its senior management of the status of major incidents for timely decision-making.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.5

controls.title: Incident Communications Plan
controls.description: Communications plan for customer notification during incidents.
controls.full_text: A communications plan covering processes to apprise customers and handle media queries should be maintained.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.6
evidence_requirements: Spokesperson and subject matter expert designations

controls.title: Customer Incident Updates
controls.description: Timely updates to customers on incident management progress.
controls.full_text: The FI should provide timely updates to customers on incident management progress and protective measures.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.7.7
evidence_requirements: Records of customer notifications during incidents

controls.title: Problem Management Process
controls.description: Problem management to determine and resolve root cause of incidents.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish problem management process and procedures to determine and resolve the root cause of incidents.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.8.1

controls.title: Incident Lessons Learned
controls.description: Record of past incidents with lessons learnt.
controls.full_text: The FI should maintain a record of past incidents which include lessons learnt to facilitate diagnosis of future incidents.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.8.2
evidence_requirements: Post-incident review reports with lessons learned
Known error database or knowledge base

controls.title: Incident Trend Analysis
controls.description: Trend analysis of past incidents to identify patterns.
controls.full_text: A trend analysis of past incidents should be performed to identify commonalities and patterns, and verify root causes were properly resolved.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 7.8.3

controls.title: System Availability Design
controls.description: IT systems designed for availability commensurate with business needs.
controls.full_text: IT systems should be designed and implemented to achieve the level of system availability that is commensurate with its business needs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.1.1

controls.title: System Redundancy
controls.description: Redundancy/fault-tolerant solutions, periodic architecture review.
controls.full_text: Redundancy or fault-tolerant solutions should be implemented for IT systems which require high availability. Periodic review of architecture to identify weaknesses.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.1.2
evidence_requirements: Architecture review reports identifying single points of failure

controls.title: Capacity Management
controls.description: Continuously monitor system resource utilisation against thresholds.
controls.full_text: The FI should continuously monitor the utilisation of its system resources against pre-defined thresholds.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.1.3

controls.title: Threshold Response Procedures
controls.description: Procedures for responding to threshold breaches.
controls.full_text: Procedures should be established to respond to situations when pre-defined thresholds for system resources and performance have been breached.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.1.4

controls.title: Recovery Objectives (RTO/RPO)
controls.description: Establish RTO and RPO aligned with business priorities.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish systems' recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) that are aligned to its business resumption priorities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.2.1
evidence_requirements: Business impact analysis supporting recovery objectives

controls.title: Disaster Recovery Plan
controls.description: DR plan with recovery procedures, roles, annual review.
controls.full_text: The FI's disaster recovery plan should include procedures to recover systems from various scenarios, roles and responsibilities, and be reviewed at least annually.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.2.2

controls.title: DR Plan Execution
controls.description: Follow tested and approved DR plan during recovery.
controls.full_text: During recovery, the FI should follow the established disaster recovery plan that has been tested and approved by management.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.2.3
evidence_requirements: Management approval records for DR plan

controls.title: Alternate Site Operations
controls.description: Periodically operate from recovery/alternate site.
controls.full_text: The FI should endeavour to operate from its recovery or alternate site periodically to ensure infrastructure can support business needs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.2.4
evidence_requirements: Infrastructure readiness assessment for alternate sites

controls.title: DR Plan Testing
controls.description: Regular testing of DR plan to validate effectiveness.
controls.full_text: The FI should perform regular testing of its disaster recovery plan to validate effectiveness and ensure systems meet recovery objectives.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.3.1

controls.title: DR Test Planning
controls.description: DR test plan with objectives, scope, scenarios, scripts.
controls.full_text: A disaster recovery test plan should include test objectives, scope, scenarios, test scripts, recovery procedures, and success criteria.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.3.2
evidence_requirements: DR test success criteria and measurement

controls.title: DR Test Scenarios
controls.description: Testing various disruption scenarios and recovery dependencies.
controls.full_text: Testing should comprise various plausible disruption scenarios and recovery dependencies between information assets.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.3.3

controls.title: Third Party DR Assessment
controls.description: Assess service provider DR capability and test coordinated recovery.
controls.full_text: The FI should assess service providers' disaster recovery capability and ensure arrangements are established and tested.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.3.4
evidence_requirements: Service provider DR capability assessment reports
Joint DR testing results with service providers

controls.title: Backup Strategy
controls.description: System and data backup strategy with regular backups.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a system and data backup strategy and develop a plan to perform regular backups.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.4.1
evidence_requirements: System and data backup strategy document

controls.title: Backup Lifecycle Policy
controls.description: Policy for backup data lifecycle including frequency, retention, storage.
controls.full_text: The FI should institute a policy to manage the backup data life cycle, covering frequency, retention period, storage mechanisms, and secure destruction.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.4.2

controls.title: Backup Restoration Testing
controls.description: Periodically test restoration of backups.
controls.full_text: The FI should periodically test the restoration of its system and data backups to validate effectiveness.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.4.3

controls.title: Backup Data Security
controls.description: Protect backup data from unauthorised access, store offline/offsite.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure confidential data in backup is secured (e.g. encrypted). Backup media should be stored offline or at an offsite location.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.4.4

controls.title: Data Centre Risk Assessment
controls.description: Threat and Vulnerability Risk Assessment (TVRA) for data centres.
controls.full_text: The FI should conduct a Threat and Vulnerability Risk Assessment (TVRA) for its data centres to identify vulnerabilities and establish protections.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.5.1

controls.title: DC Infrastructure Redundancy
controls.description: Adequate redundancy for DC power, network connectivity, cooling systems.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure adequate redundancy for power, network connectivity, and cooling systems to eliminate single points of failure.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.5.2
evidence_requirements: DC power, network, and cooling system specifications

controls.title: DC Fire Safety
controls.description: Fire detection and suppression devices in data centres.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement fire detection and suppression devices or systems such as smoke detectors and inert gas suppression systems.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.5.3
evidence_requirements: Fire detection and suppression system documentation
Fire system inspection and maintenance records

controls.title: DC Geographic Separation
controls.description: Secondary/DR DC geographically separated from primary.
controls.full_text: The FI's secondary or disaster recovery DC should be geographically separated from its primary DC.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.5.4

controls.title: DC Environmental Monitoring
controls.description: 24/7 monitoring of DC physical security and environmental controls.
controls.full_text: The DC's physical security and environmental controls should be monitored on a 24 by 7 basis.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.5.5
evidence_requirements: DC incident response plans and procedures

controls.title: DC Physical Access Controls
controls.description: Adequate physical access controls for data centres.
controls.full_text: The DC should have adequate physical access controls including need-to-have access, visitor procedures, secured access points, rack access restrictions.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 8.5.6
evidence_requirements: DC access logs and visitor records

controls.title: Access Control Principles
controls.description: Principles of never alone, segregation of duties, least privilege.
controls.full_text: The principles of 'never alone', 'segregation of duties', and 'least privilege' should be applied when granting staff access to information assets.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.1
evidence_requirements: Access control policy with principles defined

controls.title: User Access Provisioning
controls.description: User access management process for provisioning, changing, revoking access.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a user access management process to provision, change and revoke access rights to information assets.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.2

controls.title: Access Audit Trail
controls.description: Records of user access and management activities for audit.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure records of user access and user management activities are uniquely identified and logged for audit and investigation.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.3

controls.title: Password Policy
controls.description: Password policy and strong password controls.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a password policy and a process to enforce strong password controls for users' access to IT systems.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.4

controls.title: Multi-Factor Authentication
controls.description: MFA for sensitive system functions.
controls.full_text: Multi-factor authentication should be implemented for users with access to sensitive system functions.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.5
evidence_requirements: MFA coverage assessment for sensitive systems

controls.title: Periodic Access Review
controls.description: Periodic user access review by asset owners.
controls.full_text: Appropriate parties should perform periodic user access review to verify appropriateness of privileges.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.6

controls.title: Access Revocation
controls.description: Access rights revoked/disabled on change of job or termination.
controls.full_text: Access rights that are no longer needed should be revoked or disabled promptly.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.7
evidence_requirements: Timeliness records for access removal on termination

controls.title: Service Provider Access Controls
controls.description: Service providers subject to same monitoring and access restrictions.
controls.full_text: The FI should subject service providers who are given access to the FI's information assets to the same monitoring and access restrictions.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.1.8

controls.title: Privileged Access Management
controls.description: Privileged access on need-to-use basis, logged and reviewed.
controls.full_text: Access to privileged accounts should only be granted on a need-to-use basis; activities should be logged and reviewed.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.2.1
evidence_requirements: Privileged access management policy and procedures
Privileged account activity logs and review records

controls.title: System and Service Account Management
controls.description: Manage and monitor system and service accounts.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a process to manage and monitor the use of system and service accounts for suspicious activities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.2.2
evidence_requirements: Service account monitoring and review records

controls.title: Remote Access Security
controls.description: Remote connections encrypted, MFA implemented.
controls.full_text: Remote connections should be encrypted. Strong authentication such as MFA should be implemented for remote access.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.3.1
evidence_requirements: Remote access encryption and MFA configuration records

controls.title: Remote Device Security
controls.description: Remote access only from secured devices.
controls.full_text: Remote access should only be allowed from devices that have been secured according to the FI's security standards.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 9.3.2

controls.title: Cryptographic Applications
controls.description: Cryptography for data confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity.
controls.full_text: The primary applications of cryptography are to protect data confidentiality, and maintain data integrity and authenticity.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.1.1
evidence_requirements: Inventory of cryptographic applications in use

controls.title: Cryptographic Standards Adoption
controls.description: Adopt algorithms from international standards with appropriate key length.
controls.full_text: The FI should adopt cryptographic algorithms from well-established international standards and select appropriate key lengths.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.1.2

controls.title: Random Number Security
controls.description: Sufficient length and randomness for cryptographic seeds.
controls.full_text: Where security depends on unpredictability of a random seed, the FI should ensure sufficient length and randomness.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.1.3

controls.title: Algorithm Testing
controls.description: Cryptographic algorithms subject to rigorous testing.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure all cryptographic algorithms used have been subject to rigorous testing or vetting.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.1.4

controls.title: Cryptographic Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor cryptanalysis developments, update algorithms as needed.
controls.full_text: The FI should monitor developments in cryptanalysis and update or change algorithms to remain resilient.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.1.5

controls.title: Key Management Policy
controls.description: Key management covering generation, distribution, renewal, revocation, recovery, expiry.
controls.full_text: Cryptographic key management policy, standards and procedures covering key generation, distribution, installation, renewal, revocation, recovery and expiry should be established.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.1

controls.title: Key Generation Security
controls.description: Keys securely generated and protected from unauthorised disclosure.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure cryptographic keys are securely generated and protected from unauthorised disclosure.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.2

controls.title: Key Lifespan Management
controls.description: Determine appropriate key lifespan based on data sensitivity and risk.
controls.full_text: The FI should determine the appropriate lifespan of each cryptographic key based on sensitivity and risk factors.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.3

controls.title: Hardware Key Storage
controls.description: Manage keys in hardened/tamper resistant systems (HSM).
controls.full_text: The FI should manage, process and store sensitive cryptographic keys in hardened and tamper resistant systems such as HSMs.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.4

controls.title: Key Distribution Security
controls.description: Keys not exposed during transmission, use out-of-band channel.
controls.full_text: Cryptographic keys should not be exposed during transmission. Keys should be distributed via out-of-band channel or secure means.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.5

controls.title: Key Diversification
controls.description: Use keys for single purpose.
controls.full_text: Cryptographic keys should be used for a single purpose. The key for encryption should be different from the one for digital signatures.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.6
evidence_requirements: Key usage policy requiring single-purpose keys

controls.title: Compromised Key Response
controls.description: Revoke and replace compromised keys.
controls.full_text: If a key is compromised, the FI should revoke and replace it and all other keys whose security could be compromised.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.7

controls.title: Key Destruction
controls.description: Secure key destruction for expired/revoked keys.
controls.full_text: When keys have expired or been revoked, the FI should use a secure key destruction method.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.8

controls.title: Key Renewal Independence
controls.description: New key generation independent of previous compromised key.
controls.full_text: When replacing a compromised key, the new key should be generated such that knowledge of the previous key cannot derive the new key.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.9

controls.title: Key Backup
controls.description: Maintain backups of cryptographic keys for recovery.
controls.full_text: The FI should maintain backups of cryptographic keys for recovery purposes and accord them high level of protection.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 10.2.10
evidence_requirements: Key backup security and recovery test records

controls.title: Data Loss Prevention
controls.description: DLP policies for data in motion, at rest, and in use.
controls.full_text: The FI should develop comprehensive data loss prevention policies covering data in motion, data at rest, and data in use.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.1
evidence_requirements: DLP tool deployment and configuration records

controls.title: Data Theft Prevention
controls.description: Measures to prevent and detect data theft and unauthorised modification.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement measures to prevent and detect data theft and unauthorised modification in systems and endpoint devices.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.2

controls.title: Endpoint Data Encryption
controls.description: Encrypt confidential data in systems and endpoints.
controls.full_text: Confidential data stored in systems and endpoint devices should be encrypted and protected by strong access controls.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.3

controls.title: Authorised Storage Controls
controls.description: Only authorised storage media for confidential data.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure only authorised data storage media, systems and endpoint devices are used for confidential data.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.4

controls.title: Unauthorised Internet Service Controls
controls.description: Prevent unauthorised internet services for confidential data.
controls.full_text: Security measures should prevent and detect use of unauthorised internet services for communicating or storing confidential data.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.5
evidence_requirements: Web filtering and cloud access security controls

controls.title: Production Data Protection
controls.description: Restrict production data in non-production environments.
controls.full_text: The use of sensitive production data in non-production environments should be restricted with proper approval and data masking.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.6
evidence_requirements: Production data usage policy for non-production environments

controls.title: Secure Data Disposal
controls.description: Irrevocably delete confidential data before disposal/redeployment.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure confidential data is irrevocably deleted from storage media before disposal or redeployment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.1.7

controls.title: Perimeter Security
controls.description: Network security devices (firewalls) between FI and Internet.
controls.full_text: The FI should install network security devices such as firewalls to secure the network between the FI and the Internet.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.1

controls.title: Network Segmentation
controls.description: Segment networks based on criticality and data sensitivity.
controls.full_text: The FI should deploy effective security mechanisms to protect information assets. Assets should be grouped into network segments based on criticality and sensitivity.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.2

controls.title: Intrusion Prevention
controls.description: Deploy network intrusion prevention systems.
controls.full_text: Network intrusion prevention systems should be deployed to detect and block malicious network traffic.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.3

controls.title: Network Access Control
controls.description: Detect and prevent unauthorised devices on network.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement network access controls to detect and prevent unauthorised devices from connecting to its network.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.4

controls.title: Network Rule Review
controls.description: Regular review of network access control rules.
controls.full_text: Network access control rules should be reviewed regularly to ensure they are up-to-date. Obsolete rules should be removed promptly.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.5

controls.title: Web Browsing Isolation
controls.description: Isolate internet browsing from endpoint devices.
controls.full_text: The FI should consider isolating internet web browsing activities from endpoint devices through physical or logical controls.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.6

controls.title: DoS Protection
controls.description: Implement DoS protection to detect and respond to attacks.
controls.full_text: An effective DoS protection should be implemented to detect and respond to various types of DoS attacks.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.7

controls.title: Network Architecture Review
controls.description: Periodic review of network architecture for vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: A review of the FI's network architecture should be conducted periodically to identify potential cyber security vulnerabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.2.8

controls.title: System Hardening Standards
controls.description: Security standards for hardware and software configurations.
controls.full_text: Security standards for hardware and software should outline configurations that minimise exposure to cyber threats.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.1

controls.title: Configuration Compliance
controls.description: Verify standards applied uniformly, address deviations.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a process to verify standards are applied uniformly and identify deviations.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.2

controls.title: Endpoint Protection
controls.description: Endpoint protection including behavioural and signature-based solutions.
controls.full_text: Endpoint protection including behavioural-based and signature-based solutions should be implemented against malware.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.3

controls.title: Anti-Malware Management
controls.description: Keep anti-malware signatures up-to-date, regular scanning.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure anti-malware signatures are kept up-to-date and systems are regularly scanned.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.4

controls.title: Threat Detection and Response
controls.description: Detection and response for IOCs, monitor for anomalies.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement detection and response mechanisms for indicators of compromise and proactively monitor for anomalies.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.5

controls.title: Application Whitelisting
controls.description: Application white-listing for authorised software only.
controls.full_text: Security measures such as application white-listing should ensure only authorised software is allowed.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.6

controls.title: BYOD Security
controls.description: Comprehensive risk assessment and security measures for BYOD.
controls.full_text: The FI should conduct a comprehensive risk assessment and implement appropriate measures to secure its BYOD environment.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.3.7
evidence_requirements: BYOD security policy and controls documentation

controls.title: Virtualisation Security Standards
controls.description: Security standards for all virtualisation components.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure security standards are established for all components of a virtualisation solution.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.4.1

controls.title: Hypervisor Access Control
controls.description: Strong access controls for hypervisor and host OS.
controls.full_text: Strong access controls should be implemented to restrict administrative access to the hypervisor and host operating system.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.4.2
evidence_requirements: Administrative access review for virtual environments

controls.title: Virtual Image Management
controls.description: Policies for managing virtual images and snapshots.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish policies and standards to manage virtual images and snapshots securely.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.4.3

controls.title: IoT Device Inventory
controls.description: Maintain inventory of all IoT devices.
controls.full_text: The FI should maintain an inventory of all its IoT devices, including network connections and physical locations.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.5.1

controls.title: IoT Risk Controls
controls.description: Assess and implement controls for IoT risks.
controls.full_text: The FI should assess and implement processes and controls to mitigate risks arising from IoT.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.5.2

controls.title: IoT Network Security
controls.description: Secure IoT network, host in separate network segment.
controls.full_text: IoT devices should be hosted in a separate network segment from the network hosting the FI's systems and confidential data.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.5.3

controls.title: IoT Access Control
controls.description: Prevent unauthorised access to IoT devices.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement controls to prevent unauthorised access to IoT devices.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.5.4

controls.title: IoT Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor IoT devices for suspicious activities.
controls.full_text: The FI should monitor IoT devices for suspicious or anomalous system activities to isolate compromised devices promptly.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 11.5.5
evidence_requirements: IoT anomaly detection and response records

controls.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence
controls.description: Collect, process, and analyse cyber-related information.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a process to collect, process and analyse cyber-related information for relevance and potential impact.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.1.1

controls.title: Threat Information Sharing
controls.description: Procure cyber intelligence monitoring, participate in information sharing.
controls.full_text: The FI should procure cyber intelligence monitoring services and actively participate in threat information-sharing arrangements.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.1.2
evidence_requirements: Participation records in threat information sharing

controls.title: Misinformation Response
controls.description: Detect and respond to misinformation related to the FI.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a process to detect and respond to misinformation propagated via the Internet.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.1.3

controls.title: Security Operations Centre
controls.description: SOC or managed security services for continuous monitoring.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a security operations centre or acquire managed security services for continuous monitoring and analysis.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.2.1

controls.title: System Log Management
controls.description: Collect, process, review, retain system logs.
controls.full_text: A process to collect, process, review and retain system logs should be established. Logs should be protected from unauthorised access.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.2.2
evidence_requirements: Log retention and protection configuration records

controls.title: Baseline Activity Profiles
controls.description: Establish baseline profiles of system activities for anomaly detection.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a baseline profile of each IT system's routine activities and analyse against baseline profiles.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.2.3

controls.title: User Behavioural Analytics
controls.description: Apply user behavioural analytics for security monitoring.
controls.full_text: The FI should consider applying user behavioural analytics to enhance security monitoring effectiveness.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.2.4

controls.title: Event Correlation
controls.description: Correlate multiple events to identify suspicious patterns.
controls.full_text: Correlation of multiple events on system logs should be performed to identify suspicious or anomalous activity patterns.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.2.5

controls.title: Security Escalation
