controls.description: Timely escalation of suspicious activities or behaviour.
controls.full_text: A process should ensure timely escalation to relevant stakeholders regarding suspicious activities or user behaviour.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.2.6

controls.title: Cyber Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Plan to isolate, neutralise threats and resume services.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a cyber incident response and management plan to swiftly isolate and neutralise cyber threats.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.3.1
evidence_requirements: Response plan testing and exercise records

controls.title: Security Breach Investigation
controls.description: Investigate to identify security deficiencies from breaches.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a process to investigate and identify security or control deficiencies that resulted in a security breach.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.3.2

controls.title: Incident Improvement
controls.description: Use cyber intelligence and lessons learnt to enhance controls.
controls.full_text: Information from cyber intelligence and lessons learnt should be used to enhance existing controls or improve the cyber incident management plan.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 12.3.3
evidence_requirements: Control enhancement records based on incident lessons

controls.title: Vulnerability Assessment Process
controls.description: Regular vulnerability assessment of IT systems.
controls.full_text: The FI should establish a process to conduct regular vulnerability assessment on IT systems.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.1.1

controls.title: VA Scope
controls.description: VA scope covering vulnerability discovery, weak configs, open ports, web vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: VA scope should include vulnerability discovery, identification of weak security configurations, open ports, and web-based vulnerabilities.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.1.2
evidence_requirements: VA scan configuration and coverage reports

controls.title: Penetration Testing
controls.description: PT for in-depth evaluation with blackbox and greybox testing.
controls.full_text: The FI should carry out penetration testing to obtain an in-depth evaluation of cyber security defences.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.2.1

controls.title: Bug Bounty Programme
controls.description: Bug bounty to complement penetration testing.
controls.full_text: The FI may consider conducting a bug bounty programme to discover vulnerabilities by ethical hackers.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.2.2
evidence_requirements: Bug bounty findings and remediation records

controls.title: Production Environment PT
controls.description: PT on production environment with safeguards.
controls.full_text: PT should be conducted on the production environment with proper safeguards.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.2.3

controls.title: PT Frequency
controls.description: PT frequency based on system criticality and exposure.
controls.full_text: PT frequency should be determined based on system criticality and exposure to cyber risks. Internet-facing systems should be tested annually.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.2.4
evidence_requirements: PT frequency schedule based on risk classification
Annual PT completion records for internet-facing systems

controls.title: Cyber Exercises
controls.description: Regular scenario-based cyber exercises.
controls.full_text: The FI should carry out regular scenario-based cyber exercises to validate response and recovery plans.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.3.1

controls.title: Exercise Stakeholder Involvement
controls.description: Involve relevant stakeholders in cyber exercises.
controls.full_text: Relevant stakeholders including senior management and business functions should participate in exercises.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.3.2

controls.title: Adversarial Attack Simulation
controls.description: Red team exercise to validate cyber defence.
controls.full_text: The FI should perform adversarial attack simulation exercise to test and validate the effectiveness of its cyber defence.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.4.1

controls.title: Exercise Rules of Engagement
controls.description: Define objectives, scope, rules of engagement.
controls.full_text: Objectives, scope and rules of engagement should be defined before the exercise, conducted under close supervision.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.4.2

controls.title: Intelligence-Based Scenarios
controls.description: Design realistic threat scenarios based on plausible cyber threats.
controls.full_text: The threat scenario should be designed based on challenging but plausible cyber threats.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.5.1
evidence_requirements: Threat intelligence used for scenario development

controls.title: Threat Actor Analysis
controls.description: Design scenarios using threat intelligence relevant to IT environment.
controls.full_text: The FI could design exercise scenarios using threat intelligence to identify threat actors and their techniques.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.5.2

controls.title: Remediation Management
controls.description: Comprehensive remediation process with severity assessment and timeframes.
controls.full_text: A comprehensive remediation process should include severity assessment, classification, timeframes, and risk management strategies.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 13.6.1
evidence_requirements: Remediation tracking reports with severity and timelines

controls.title: Online Financial Services Security
controls.description: Security measures commensurate with risk for online services.
controls.full_text: In delivering online financial services, the FI should implement security and control measures commensurate with the risk.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.1
evidence_requirements: Online services risk and control documentation

controls.title: Communication Channel Security
controls.description: Secure channels with encryption and digital signatures.
controls.full_text: The FI should secure its communications channels to protect customer data through encryption and digital signatures.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.2

controls.title: Web Application Security
controls.description: Minimise exposure to common attack vectors.
controls.full_text: Adequate measures should be taken to minimise exposure to common attack vectors such as code injection, XSS, MITMA, DDoS.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.3

controls.title: Mobile Application Risk Management
controls.description: Address risks unique to mobile applications.
controls.full_text: An FI offering services via mobile devices should be aware of and address risks unique to mobile applications.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.4

controls.title: Secure App Distribution
controls.description: Apps only through official stores or secure channels.
controls.full_text: The FI should only make available mobile applications through official mobile application stores or secure channels.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.5

controls.title: Anti-Phishing Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor for phishing campaigns, take immediate action.
controls.full_text: The FI should actively monitor for phishing campaigns and take immediate action to report and remove malicious content.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.6

controls.title: Rooted/Jailbroken Device Controls
controls.description: Disallow rooted/jailbroken devices unless application sandboxed.
controls.full_text: Rooted or jailbroken devices should be disallowed unless the application has been secured within a sandbox.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.1.7

controls.title: Online MFA
controls.description: Multi-factor authentication for online financial services login.
controls.full_text: Multi-factor authentication should be deployed at login for online financial services.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.1

controls.title: Password Transmission Security
controls.description: End-to-end encryption for customer password transmission.
controls.full_text: End-to-end encryption should be implemented for transmission of customer passwords.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.2

controls.title: Transaction Signing
controls.description: Digital signatures for high-risk activities.
controls.full_text: Transaction-signing should be implemented for authorising high-risk activities to protect integrity of customer data.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.3

controls.title: Adaptive Authentication
controls.description: Risk-based or adaptive authentication options.
controls.full_text: The FI may implement risk-based or adaptive authentication commensurate with transaction risk level.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.4

controls.title: OTP Validity Period
controls.description: OTP validity period as short as practicable.
controls.full_text: When implementing time-based OTPs, the validity period should be as short as practicable.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.5

controls.title: Biometric Data Protection
controls.description: Encrypt biometric data and authentication credentials.
controls.full_text: Biometrics-related data and authentication credentials should be encrypted in storage and during transmission.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.6

controls.title: Biometric Performance Calibration
controls.description: Biometric solution performance calibrated to risk.
controls.full_text: The biometric solution performance should be calibrated to be commensurate with the risk.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.7

controls.title: Soft Token Security
controls.description: Soft token provisioning with identity verification and device binding.
controls.full_text: Appropriate measures including identity verification and device binding should be implemented during soft token provisioning.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.8

controls.title: Session Management
controls.description: Session integrity and termination after inactivity.
controls.full_text: The authenticated session should remain intact throughout interaction. Sessions should auto-terminate after inactivity.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.9
evidence_requirements: Session timeout and hijack detection settings

controls.title: Corporate Customer Controls
controls.description: Alternate controls for corporate customers with security assessment.
controls.full_text: Where alternate controls are implemented for corporate customers, a security risk assessment should be performed.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.10

controls.title: Credential Storage Security
controls.description: Store authentication credentials in tamper-resistant form.
controls.full_text: Authentication credentials should be stored in a form that is resistant to reverse engineering.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.2.11

controls.title: Real-Time Fraud Monitoring
controls.description: Real-time fraud monitoring to detect and block suspicious transactions.
controls.full_text: The FI should implement real-time fraud monitoring systems to identify and block suspicious or fraudulent transactions.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.3.1

controls.title: Suspicious Transaction Investigation
controls.description: Process to investigate suspicious transactions.
controls.full_text: A process should be established to investigate suspicious transactions and ensure issues are promptly addressed.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.3.2

controls.title: Customer Suspicious Activity Notification
controls.description: Notify customers of suspicious activities above threshold.
controls.full_text: The FI should notify customers of suspicious activities or funds transfers above defined thresholds.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.3.3
evidence_requirements: Customer notification procedures for suspicious activities

controls.title: Customer Security Education
controls.description: Inform customers of security best practices for online services.
controls.full_text: Customers should be informed of security best practices when using online financial services.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.4.1

controls.title: Cyber Threat Customer Alerts
controls.description: Alert customers of new cyber threats timely.
controls.full_text: The FI should alert customers on a timely basis to new cyber threats.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.4.2

controls.title: Unauthorised Transaction Detection Guidance
controls.description: Advise customers on detecting unauthorised transactions.
controls.full_text: The FI should advise customers on means to detect unauthorised transactions and report suspected fraud.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 14.4.3

controls.title: IT Audit Function
controls.description: IT audit for board assurance on risk management effectiveness.
controls.full_text: Audit plays an important role to assess the effectiveness of controls, risk management and governance. IT audit should provide the board an independent opinion.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 15.1.1
evidence_requirements: IT audit charter and function documentation
IT audit reports presented to board

controls.title: Auditable Areas
controls.description: Comprehensive auditable areas for technology risk.
controls.full_text: A comprehensive set of auditable areas for technology risk should be identified for effective risk assessment during audit planning.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 15.1.2

controls.title: IT Audit Frequency
controls.description: IT audit frequency based on criticality and risk.
controls.full_text: The frequency of IT audits should be commensurate with the criticality and risk posed by the information asset.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 15.1.3
evidence_requirements: IT audit schedule based on risk assessment

controls.title: IT Auditor Competency
controls.description: IT auditors with requisite competency and skills.
controls.full_text: The FI should ensure its IT auditors have the requisite competency and skills to effectively assess IT controls.
controls.ai_notes: Extracted from MAS TRM Guidelines Section 15.1.4
evidence_requirements: IT auditor qualification and certification records
